paper_id: Borges et al. - 2022 - A Multi-level Ontology-based Approach for Descriptors of Catalogued Resources
input_limitations: Markdown extraction contains mojibake in some names/symbols and figures are represented only as [FIGURE], but the relevant prose sections are readable.

models.model_label: MLT-UFO-based DCAT core ontology for catalogued resource descriptors

evidence_quotes.quote: The proposed model aims to make the DCAT structures  adherent  to  the  reality  aspects  that  need  to  be  represented,  establishing ontological commitments in an explicit way, which helps the understanding of those who will use it.
Therefore, the second-order domain types establish, together with DCAT concepts, a language structure for understanding the catalogue organization.
These aspects help modelers better understand domain concepts, providing ambiguity handling and defining constraints to better align them to reality.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the model as clarifying DCAT concepts, handling ambiguity, and supporting understanding of catalogue organization and domain concepts.

evidence_quotes.quote: It can provide a canonical model for specific implementations, to be adopted by repositories and by Web resource search engines and analysis mechanisms.
This section provides an overview of applying the MLT-DCAT model to define resource descriptors for  a  catalogue  representing  a  DSpace  repository.

Thus, ontologies based on this canonical model can extend the interoperability mechanisms of digital platforms to repositories, maintaining compliance with protocols such as OAI-PMH and making RDF information available to search engines and retrieval agents.

goals.justification: The model is not reported as a deployed system, but the paper states an intended implementation/integration role and demonstrates applying it to define repository descriptors.

evidence_quotes.quote: The  presented  CQs  assist  administrators  in  managing  the  catalogue.
For  agents  (human  and machine),  they  allow  checking  descriptor  types  and  acceptable  standards  before  submission  for publication.
Summarizing this section, modeling with categorized types establishes conformance constraints that domain users must respect.

goals.justification: The competency questions and conformance constraints are presented as aids for catalogue management and checking standards before publication, which supports problem-solving and operational decisions.

evidence_quotes.quote: The second refers to the availability of the RDF model associated with the data that provides the agents (humans or machines) the knowledge of the catalogue structure and the standardized terms adopted, contributing to interoperability mechanisms.
As previously mentioned, the model, in RDF, would make available the structure adopted in the catalogue,  as  well  as  the  standardized  terms  (semantic  artifacts).
In addition, these standards are available for agent access, contributing to understanding the catalogue structure.

goals.justification: The model is described as a persistent RDF representation that makes catalogue structure and standardized terms available for later access by agents, fitting asynchronous documentation/reference use.

evidence_quotes.quote: Analyzing these metadata allows us to identify that they constitute a domain of descriptors that need a consistent conceptualization, explicitly and formally represented, aiming at sharing the concepts involved.

uncertain_goals.reason: The paper mentions sharing concepts, but does not describe live interaction, explanation, alignment, or communication between distinct stakeholder groups.
